---
title: "Research"
toc: false
aliases:
  - publications.html
  - software.html
---

## Publications

::: {.pub-entry}
[Extractive versus Generative Language Models for Political Conflict Text Classification]{.pub-title}
[with P.T. Brandt, S. Alsarra, V. D'Orazio, D. Heintze, L. Khan, J. Osorio & M. Sianan · *Political Analysis*, 2025]{.pub-meta}
[[Article](https://doi.org/10.1017/pan.2025.10027) [Code](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/KDO5AM) [Slides](research/ConfliBERT_Presentation.pdf)]{.pub-links}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[ConflLlama: Domain-Specific Adaptation of Large Language Models for Conflict Event Classification]{.pub-title}
[with P.T. Brandt · *Research & Politics*, 2025]{.pub-meta}
[[Article](https://doi.org/10.1177/20531680251356282) [Code](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/0MUGE6) [Slides](research/APSA___ConflLlama.pdf)]{.pub-links}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Public Health Advocacy in Times of Pandemic: An Analysis of the Medicare-For-All Debate on Twitter During COVID-19]{.pub-title}
[with S. Kumar & P. Zhang · *Behavioral Sciences*, 2025]{.pub-meta}
[[Article](https://doi.org/10.3390/bs15020223) [Code](https://doi.org/10.7910/DVN/OWVD70)]{.pub-links}
:::

## Under Review

::: {.pub-entry}
[Electoral Accountability and the Channeling of Content Removal: Theory and Evidence from Google Transparency Reports]{.pub-title}
[with P. Zhang]{.pub-meta}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Framework Laws and Fading Oversight: How the Dutch Executive Expanded Its Lawmaking Authority]{.pub-title}
[with C. Egger & A. Zhelyazkova · Revise & resubmit]{.pub-meta}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Selective Scrutiny: Single-Party Majority and the Strategic Withdrawal of Parliamentary Oversight in India]{.pub-title}
[[Draft](research/Meher_2025_Legislating_Without_Scrutiny.pdf)]{.pub-links}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Build, Borrow, or Just Fine-Tune? Empirical Guidelines for NLP Model Selection in Computational Social Science]{.pub-title}
[[Draft](research/Meher_Build_Borrow.pdf) [Model](https://huggingface.co/shreyasmeher/Confli-mBERT)]{.pub-links}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
["Why Aren't We Talking About This?" The Attention Trap in Networked Protest]{.pub-title}
[[Draft](research/Meher_2025_Attention_Trap.pdf)]{.pub-links}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Silencing the Silencing: Media Amplification of Internet Shutdowns in India]{.pub-title}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Two Types of Censorship? An Assessment of the Informational Autocracy Thesis]{.pub-title}
[with P. Zhang]{.pub-meta}
:::

## Working Papers

::: {.pub-entry}
[Building Capacity or Building Checks? What EU Enlargement Reports Emphasise and Democratic Backsliding]{.pub-title}
[with A. Zhelyazkova & C. Egger · EPSS 2026, Belfast]{.pub-meta}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Strategic Anticipation and Expert Review: The Determinants of Critical Advisory Opinions from the Dutch Council of State]{.pub-title}
[with C. Egger & A. Zhelyazkova]{.pub-meta}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[On Time, but Empty: How Dutch Ministers Answer Their Critics]{.pub-title}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Between the Documents: Unit Choice in Corpus-Scale Political Text Analysis]{.pub-title}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Using Messy Text in Future LLM Annotations]{.pub-title}
[with P.T. Brandt, P. Cuellar-Cuellar & X. Zhao]{.pub-meta}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Event Horizon: Revolutionizing Data Annotation with Reinforcement Learning]{.pub-title}
[[Model](https://huggingface.co/shreyasmeher/Qwen-GLOCON-Reasoning)]{.pub-links}
:::

## Software

::: {.pub-entry}
[ConfliBERT GUI]{.pub-title}
[Browser-based NLP toolkit for conflict and political violence text analysis: NER, classification, question answering, and LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning.]{.pub-meta}
[[GitHub](https://github.com/shreyasmeher/ConfliBERT-gui)]{.pub-links}
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[conflibertR]{.pub-title}
[R package wrapping ConfliBERT for inference, fine-tuning, benchmarking, and model comparison.]{.pub-meta}
[[GitHub](https://github.com/shreyasmeher/conflibertR)]{.pub-links}
:::

## Dissertation

::: {.pub-entry}
[Digital Sovereignty: The Political Economy of Internet Governance]{.pub-title}
[The University of Texas at Dallas, 2025]{.pub-meta}
[[Slides](research/Dissertation_March-31.pdf)]{.pub-links}
:::
